content.introduction: Selection sort repeatedly selects the minimum from the unsorted region and places it at the next sorted position.

sections.title: 7.3.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define sorted/unsorted partition invariant and minimum-selection logic per pass.
examples: Track minimum index during each pass
Contrast swap frequency with bubble sort

sections.title: 7.3.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe scanning for minimum index and performing one swap at the end of each pass.
examples: Swap current position with discovered minimum

sections.title: 7.3.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(n^2) regardless of input order, with O(1) space and low swap count.
examples: Use selection when minimizing swaps is important
Compare non-adaptive nature with insertion sort

keyTakeaways: Benefit from low swap count in some contexts
Use primarily for small-scale tasks and pedagogy
